raw_response:
Below are short, targeted notes from England. I will be calm and clear. My aim: avoid needless Anglo‑French bloodletting in the Channel, keep the North Sea safe for commerce, and prevent any continental power from becoming dominant by flexible bargaining. I offer clear forecasts about likely fleet risks so we can build sensible coalitions.

To France (private)
I value a straightforward, practical understanding rather than an early clash. Forecast: two fleets in the Channel/North Sea (BRE vs LON/EDI) will turn the opening into a naval brawl that benefits Germany on the continent. Proposal: you keep Brest quiet (hold or southward), and I will keep London/Edinburgh out of the English Channel (I will instead secure the North Sea / Irish approaches). In return I will not contest your moves into Burgundy/Spain and will discourage Germany from pushing westward. If Germany shows naval ambition in the Heligoland/Skagerrak area, I will act decisively to check them — but I prefer we not waste ships fighting each other first. Agree?

To Germany (private)
I prefer a stable North Sea rather than immediate hostility. Forecast: an unchecked German presence on the Dutch/Baltic gateway (Heligoland/Denmark) will make an Anglo‑French reaction likely and dangerous for you. Proposal: respect Belgium/Netherlands and avoid biting at Holland/Belgium from the outset; I will not actively oppose reasonable German continental moves so long as you do not attempt naval domination of the North Sea. If you wish, we can share intelligence about fleet movements in the Heligoland/Skagerrak sector to avoid accidents.

To Russia (private)
You command a strong eastern front and your fleets do not threaten my islands directly — unless Norway becomes contested. Forecast: a Russian move for Norway or Sweden will force my fleets north and complicate my willingness to check Germany. Proposal: I will remain neutral toward your southern interests if you leave Norway/Sweden out of the opening; mutual non‑interference in the North Atlantic and Norwegian littoral will benefit us both. If Germany presses west, I will be willing to coordinate diplomatically against continental overreach.

To Italy (private)
I do not expect to be your enemy and have no appetite for Mediterranean adventures right now. Forecast: Italian naval moves in the western Med that support France could draw me westward; I prefer that not happen early. Proposal: let us remain indifferent to each other — you focus on Italy/Adriatics and I will steer clear of Provence/Brest contests. If you see value in keeping the Mediterranean stable, we have common cause.

To Austria and Turkey (joint / separate)
I have no direct quarrel with either of you. Forecast: any large push into the Balkans that stabilizes or unifies a single continental bloc will force others to react. Proposal: keep your arrangements local; I will not interfere in the Adriatic/Mediterranean if you avoid entangling alliances seeking continental hegemony. If Austria or Turkey need a maritime friend to safeguard trade, I can discuss limited cooperation.

General note to all parties
My near‑term naval posture will be conservative but watchful: I shall prioritize securing safe lanes in the Irish Sea and the North Sea and will reserve the right to act if another power seeks naval primacy in those waters. If you want stable seas, suggest clear limits for fleet movements in the Channel/Skagerrak/Heligoland corridors now — it will reduce accidents and allow us to focus on real threats on land. I prefer flexible agreements that keep the balance of power intact rather than rigid blocs.

If anyone wants specifics (I will tell you my likely spring moves if it helps shore up an agreement), ask and I will be candid. — England
